title: 3 Ordering and Replacing Hard Tokens
body_text:
3	Ordering and Replacing Hard Tokens
3.1	Ordering Hard Tokens
Your financial institution is responsible for ordering hard tokens. User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) order hard tokens using the Order Hard Tokens request type in ServiceNow. Hard tokens are shipped to your financial institution by the vendor. Contact Hypersecu for hard token invoicing and shipping inquiries. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites and Other Resources.
Note
:	See the User Management Part for procedures for assigning a hard token to a user.
Order hard tokens for 2-step security as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow.
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Selecting the Service Catalogue
Select
Central 1 Client Self Service
, and then select
Order Hard Tokens
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Selecting Order Hard Tokens
Complete the Hard Token Order form, then select
Submit
. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 6.
Note:	Contact Hypersecu for hard token invoicing and shipping inquiries. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites and Other Resources.
Figure 6: Submitting the Hard Token Order Form
3.2	Replacing Hard Tokens
You must replace lost, damaged, or expired hard tokens. The lifespan of a hard token battery is approximately three years but that may vary based on token use. We recommend replacing a hard token three years after the order date. When a userâs hard token is registered in the User Management application, the recommended token replacement date is available on the userâs 2-step security summary. See the User Management Part for more information.
END
